excerpts:
Section Title: Key Drivers of Gold > Go for the Gold
Content:
We advocate for a 10% weighting in gold and gold-related securities, with 5% in bullion (bars, coins, jewelry) and the other 5% in high-quality gold mining stocks and ETFs.
 ... 
Section Title: Key Drivers of Gold > Go for the Gold > Quantitative Modeling
Content:
GOAU took us thousands of hours of research and backtesting to develop, using a quant model to screen for the strongest mining stocks
Section Title: Key Drivers of Gold > Smart Beta 2.0
Content:
With over 30 years’ experience in the precious metals and mining space, CEO and chief investment officer Frank Holmes took his gold knowledge and blended it with a quant model to create a dynamic, rules-based ETF in 2017.
GOAU is different because it has a smart beta 2.0 strategy. Unlike many other gold mining funds and ETFs, ours is not based strictly on market cap. Instead, it takes a quantitative approach, seeking to invest in high-quality, well-managed producers that have a proven track record of sustainable profitability.
Section Title: Key Drivers of Gold > Ready to Invest? Here’s How
Content:
In order to purchase one of U.S. Global Investors’ ETF products, you will need to start by opening a brokerage account with a broker-dealer. If you already have a brokerage account, simply contact your broker-dealer and let them know the ticker symbol of the ETF you are interested in. You can also make a purchase online through your broker’s website. Simply find the area labeled for trading stocks and ETFs and enter the ticker symbol of your choice.
Unlike mutual funds, exchange traded funds can trade throughout the day just like a stock. Because you buy and sell shares of an ETF on an exchange, price is determined by supply and demand throughout the day, not just at the end of the trading day.
U.S. Global Investors’ ETF products are available through various channels including broker-dealers, investment advisers, and other financial services firms, including:
[](https://robinhood.com/us/en/stocks/GOAU/)

excerpts:
**U.S. Global GO GOLD and Precious Metal Miners ETF (GOAU)**
**Methodology Revision, Effective March 2020**
The Index universe consists of the common stock or ADRs of Precious Metals Companies across the
globe that earn at least 50 percent of their aggregate revenue from precious metals through active (i.e.,
mining or production) or passive (i.e., owning royalties or production streams) means. The universe of
Precious Metals Companies is screened for investibility (e.g., must be listed on a securities exchange)
and liquidity (minimum average daily value traded). Precious Metals Companies that rely primarily on
debt to finance their business are eliminated from the Index. To qualify for inclusion in the Index,
Precious Metals Companies must have their common stock or a sponsored ADR listed on one of the
following exchanges: Toronto Stock Exchange, TSX Venture, New York Stock Exchange (main market

results.title: OneGold™ | GOAU ETF | US Global GO Gold And Precious Metal Miners ETF
excerpts:
Section Title: U.S. Global GO Gold And Precious Metal Miners ETF
Content:
GOAU
 ... 
Section Title: $50.24 > GOAU Overview
Content:
The GOAU Gold ETF tracks an index comprising 28 precious metal mining firms listed in major global markets including the US, Canada, Australia, Hong Kong, and South Africa. Unlike many competitors, GOAU applies a unique screening process based on fundamental factors and employs a complex tiered weighting structure. This approach includes royalty and streaming companies alongside traditional miners, distinguishing it within the sector.
Section Title: $50.24 > GOAU Overview
Content:
The ETF starts by excluding firms primarily financed with debt and prioritizes miners with strong revenue per employee metrics. GOAU's portfolio is structured into four fixed tiers, with Tier 1 comprising three top-priority companies heavily weighted, and Tier 4 consisting of smaller firms with broader geographic listings, reconstituting its holdings quarterly. This strategy provides investors with diversified exposure to the precious metals mining industry while emphasizing financial health and operational efficiency metrics in its selection criteria.
